news.summary: In Los Angeles on May 23, 2025, Bitcoin reached a record high of $109,400, signaling a positive outlook for the cryptocurrency market. This surge follows improvements in the global economic landscape and a temporary trade deal between the US and China. Bitcoin has grown by over 26% in the last month, sparking anticipation among analysts for further increases in the $114,000–$120,000 range. As the demand for digital assets rises, cloud mining service provider PAIRMiner has noted an increase in interest. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bitcoin reaches all-time high of $109,400 amidst global easing tensions and China-US trade deal. Interest in cloud mining rises.

news.summary: Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) has seen a growth spurt, with a 2.70% increase in a day, trading at $180.53. It has given 4.49% weekly and 18.97% monthly returns. This surge follows a partnership with R3, a corporate blockchain giant, known for Corda. This partnership aims to bridge traditional finance with permissionless blockchain tech, developing an enterprise-grade service on Solana's Layer-1 blockchain. R3's move into public blockchain infrastructure for tokenizing real-world assets marks a shift from serving banks with private chains. Solana Foundation President Lily Liu joined R3's board, emphasizing the significance of this partnership.
news.headline: Solana gains 2.70% trading at $180.53, +4.49% weekly and +18.97% monthly. Partners with R3 for blockchain development.

news.summary: The US Department of Justice filed a civil forfeiture complaint to seize over $24 million in cryptocurrency from Rustam Rafailevich Gallyamov, a Russian national accused of developing the Qakbot malware. Gallyamov, a 48-year-old from Moscow, is allegedly the malware developer behind the Qakbot botnet. The DOJ announced the charges against him, emphasizing their determination to hold cybercriminals accountable and use legal tools to identify, charge, and disrupt criminal activity. The indictment states that Gallyamov operated Qakbot as early as 2008, infecting thousands of computers to establish a botnet, which was later used by others to deploy ransomware like Prolock, Dopplepaymer, Egregor, REvil, Conti, and others. Despite a 2023 operation that disrupted the Qakbot botnet and seized over 170 Bitcoin, $4 million in USDt, and USDC stablecoins from him, Gallyamov allegedly continued his criminal activities. This ongoing effort aims to identify, disrupt, and compensate victims of cybercrime, with the FBI's Los Angeles Field Office noting that Gallyamov persisted in deploying alternative methods to distribute his malware even after the initial operation.
news.headline: DOJ seizes $24M in crypto from Qakbot malware dev; Russian national accused, indictment filed; DOJ determined to hold cybercriminals accountable.

news.summary: Ripple's cryptocurrency (XRP) surged past $2.45, reclaiming a significant psychological level, driven by factors like the launch of a U.S.-listed XRP futures ETF, a new coin burn mechanism, and positive technical indicators. The price climb started from $2.32 and witnessed a rising open interest. Additionally, the launch of a euro-denominated stablecoin on the XRP Ledger under the EU's regulatory framework added to the positive sentiment. Noteworthy developments include the introduction of the first futures-based XRP ETF by Volatility Shares on NASDAQ, with 80% asset allocation to XRP futures contracts for institutional investors seeking regulated exposure. The article also mentions trading opportunities on Plus500 and Coinbase for new traders, with rewards for completing educational courses and making qualifying trades. news.headline: Ripple (XRP) rises above $2.45 following news of XRP futures ETF launch, coin burn, and euro stablecoin addition.

news.summary: This morning, U.S. stocks experienced a decline, with the Nasdaq Composite dropping over 1% on Friday. The Dow decreased by 0.70% to 41,566.04, while the NASDAQ fell by 1.12% to 18,713.37. The S&P 500 also saw a decline, dropping by 0.87% to 5,791.25. Real estate shares fell by just 0.1%, while information technology stocks dipped by 1.4%. Workday, Inc. (NASDAQ: WDAY) shares dropped by more than 11% after releasing first-quarter results, reporting earnings of $2.23 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus estimate of $2.01 by 10.95%. Quarterly revenue stood at $2.24 billion, beating the Street estimate. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Nasdaq drops over 1%, Workday shares plummet 11% after Q1 results. Dow at 41,566.04, Nasdaq at 18,713.37, S&P at 5,791.25.

news.summary: On May 12, 2025, hackers hijacked Curve Finance's ".fi" domain through DNS manipulation and led users to a fake website, attempting to drain their wallets. This was the second attack in a week and similar to a previous one in August 2022. The attack targeted the DNS layer, not the protocol's smart contracts. Different DNS hijacking methods were explained, such as local DNS hijack and router hijack. The attackers altered DNS records with access to the registrar "iwantmyname." The backend remained unaffected, but millions were lost through a spoofed front end. Curve Finance responded by redirecting to a temporary domain, securing fund management, and initiating mitigation efforts. The hack raised concerns about the vulnerability of decentralized finance (DeFi) interfaces to DNS hijacking and highlighted the importance of bridging the gap between decentralized protocols and centralized interfaces for better security. To prevent such attacks, crypto projects can integrate decentralized DNS alternatives, use decentralized file storage systems, implement DNSSEC, secure registrar accounts, and educate users. It is crucial for the crypto industry to explore decentralized web infrastructure like IPFS and ENS for enhanced security.
news.headline: DNS hijacking occurs when hackers alter DNS records to redirect users to fake sites. Curve Finance suffered this on May 12, 2025, as attackers rerouted ".fi" domain, attempting to drain wallets. This was the second attack in a week, where users were misled to provide wallet signatures. The hack didn't breach protocols but targeted the DNS layer. DNS is vital for the internet, functioning like a phonebook. This isn't the first time Curve Finance faced such an attack. Attackers can conduct DNS hijacking through various methods like local DNS hijack, router hijack, or a man-in-the-middle attack. To deal with DNS hijacking, projects can integrate decentralized DNS alternatives, use decentralized file storage systems, implement DNSSEC, secure registrar accounts, and educate users.

news.summary: Avalanche (CRYPTO: AVAX) experienced a significant rally, surging 8.5% from trading in the low $20s to reach $25.1. Prior to this increase, the token had been stabilizing around $22 and has now reached close to $25.6. This surge follows the partnership announcement with the International Federation of Association Football (FIFA) to launch their Layer-1 blockchain on Avalanche's network. Avalanche recently introduced the Avalanche9000 upgrade, reducing costs by approximately 90%. The collaboration with FIFA is deemed as a crucial development in the blockchain infrastructure evolution by Avalanche, showcasing the network's reliability. The prominent partnership with FIFA highlights Avalanche's attractiveness in the realm of sports and enterprise blockchains.
